text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY — FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Margaret Okonkwo | DOB: [on file] | MRN: [on file]
Attending: [Oncologist]
Visit type: Established patient, routine oncology follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Routine follow-up, metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma on cabozantinib. Restaging scan review.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo is a 64-year-old woman with a history of stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right kidney, with retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy and bilateral pulmonary metastases at presentation, now approximately 18 months from original diagnosis and 9 months into second-line therapy with cabozantinib. She presents today for review of restaging CT imaging and ongoing TKI management.

Brief oncologic history: She was diagnosed with a large right renal mass (11.4 cm) with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%) and ISUP grade 3 disease. Molecular profiling (FoundationOne CDx) demonstrated VHL loss, PBRM1 deletion, BAP1 loss, SETD2 frameshift, and TP53 R248W — a high-risk genomic profile consistent with aggressive ccRCC. She underwent cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal LND approximately 18 months ago, confirming pT3a pN1 M1 disease with 5 of 8 nodes positive and extranodal extension. PD-L1 CPS was 18 on the nephrectomy specimen.

She received first-line ipi/nivo (ipilimumab 1 mg/kg + nivolumab 3 mg/kg q3w x4 cycles per CheckMate 214), complicated by Grade 2 immune colitis (managed with oral prednisone taper, ipi held x1 cycle) and Grade 2 immune-related hypothyroidism (levothyroxine initiated). Initial imaging at 4 months showed mixed response with shrinkage of dominant RLL nodule and LUL lesion; however, restaging at approximately 9 months from start of therapy demonstrated clear progression across all pulmonary sites and new liver lesions (right hepatic lobe x2). She transitioned to cabozantinib 60 mg daily at that point.

Cabozantinib was dose-reduced to 40 mg daily after cycle 1 due to Grade 2 palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia (hand-foot syndrome) and Grade 2 diarrhea. She also developed Grade 2 hypertension on cabo, managed with uptitration of her amlodipine from 5 mg to 10 mg daily. First restaging at 3 months on cabo showed stable disease with minor decreases across most pulmonary targets and stable hepatic lesions. Six-month restaging also showed continued stable disease. Today we are reviewing scans from her 9-month restaging point on cabozantinib.

Since last visit approximately 3 months ago, she reports:
- Fatigue: persistent, moderate — she describes it as "bone-tired by mid-afternoon," better on days when she takes short walks. She is limiting housework but remains independent with ADLs.
- HFS: ongoing but Grade 1 — erythema and mild tenderness of bilateral plantar surfaces, no blistering, no open areas. She is using her urea cream twice daily and wearing cushioned footwear. She has adapted well.
- Diarrhea: Grade 1, 1-2 loose stools above her baseline most days. Taking loperamide PRN (1-2 tabs 2-3x/week). Not limiting her activities.
- Nausea: mild, intermittent, typically in the morning after taking the pill. She now takes it with a small snack which has helped.
- Appetite: reduced, but she is eating. She has lost approximately 4 kg over the course of TKI therapy total; weight appears stable over the last month per home scale.
- No new neurological symptoms, no bone pain, no headaches, no visual changes.
- No hemoptysis, no shortness of breath at rest; mild dyspnea on exertion walking uphill — she attributes this to deconditioning.
- Constipation is not a current issue (hypothyroidism was contributing earlier; now resolved on levothyroxine).
- She was referred to integrative oncology at the last visit for exercise counseling; she has attended two sessions and reports finding it helpful.
- Social work follow-up is ongoing; she notes she has been having more frank discussions with her adult children about her diagnosis.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Persistent moderate fatigue as above. No fever, night sweats, or chills. Modest weight loss over TKI course (stable recently).
Cardiovascular: BP has been running 130s/80s at home per her log. No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
Pulmonary: Mild exertional dyspnea (stable), no cough, no hemoptysis, no pleuritic pain.
GI: Intermittent Grade 1 diarrhea and mild nausea as above. No hematochezia, no melena. No abdominal pain.
GU: No gross hematuria, no dysuria. Voiding normally from single left kidney.
Musculoskeletal: Mild bilateral ankle and hand stiffness in the morning (residual from prior joint symptoms on IO — currently Grade 0-1). Bilateral foot discomfort with HFS Grade 1.
Skin: HFS as above. No rash elsewhere.
Neurological: No new symptoms.
Endocrine: Tolerating levothyroxine well. No heat intolerance, no palpitations, no tremor.
Psychosocial: Anxiety related to disease trajectory — being followed by social work. Sleep somewhat disrupted.

---

PHYSICAL EXAM

Vitals: BP 134/82 mmHg (amlodipine 10 mg), HR 74, RR 16, SpO2 96% on room air, Weight 66.2 kg (down ~4 kg from TKI start, stable from last visit)
General: Alert and oriented, pleasant, in no acute distress. Appears mildly fatigued but conversant.
HEENT: No lymphadenopathy in cervical/supraclavicular chains. Oropharynx without mucositis.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. No JVD.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi. No dullness to percussion.
Abdomen: Well-healed right flank nephrectomy scar. Abdomen soft, non-tender, no organomegaly appreciable. No ascites.
Extremities: Bilateral palmar and plantar surfaces with mild erythema and scaling, Grade 1 HFS — no blistering, ulceration, or areas of breakdown. No lower extremity edema. No calf tenderness.
Neuro: Grossly intact. No focal deficits.
Skin: No other rashes, no findings suggestive of new irAE.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (from today and recent draw)

CBC:
  - WBC 5.8 K/uL (normal)
  - Hgb 10.8 g/dL (mild anemia, stable from prior)
  - Plt 218 K/uL

CMP:
  - Na 138, K 4.1, CO2 24, Cr 1.4 mg/dL (stable — chronic reduction post-nephrectomy, eGFR ~44)
  - Total bilirubin 0.7, AST 28, ALT 32, Alk Phos 88 — LFTs normal
  - Albumin 3.6 g/dL

TSH: 3.1 mIU/L (within normal range on levothyroxine 75 mcg)

Lipase: not drawn today (no abdominal symptoms)

Urinalysis: not done today

---

IMAGING RESULTS

Reviewed: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (restaging, 9 months on cabozantinib). Report reviewed in detail.

Chest findings: The right lower lobe lesion is minimally changed from the prior study, stable. The left upper lobe and right upper lobe nodules are similarly unchanged. Left lower lobe nodule stable. The right middle lobe nodules (two) unchanged. No new pulmonary nodules. No new pleural effusion.

Abdomen/pelvis: Right hepatic lobe lesions are again seen — the larger one measures approximately 0.7 cm (stable), and the smaller one approximately 0.5 cm (stable). No new hepatic lesions identified. Nephrectomy bed without evidence of locoregional recurrence. No new retroperitoneal adenopathy. Adrenal glands normal. No new peritoneal disease. No pelvic abnormalities.

Bones: No new suspicious osseous lesions. Known degenerative changes in lumbar spine.

Overall impression as per radiology: no significant interval change; all sites of known disease appear stable. No new sites of involvement. Overall picture consistent with a durable plateau on current therapy.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA — CONTINUED STABLE DISEASE ON CABOZANTINIB

Ms. Okonkwo continues to demonstrate durable stable disease on cabozantinib 40 mg daily. This is her 9-month mark on cab, and all known sites of disease remain stable — bilateral pulmonary nodules (right middle, lower; left upper, lower), right hepatic lesions — with no progression and no new lesions on today's restaging CT. This is a meaningful outcome given her high-risk features at diagnosis: grade 3 ccRCC with 20% sarcomatoid differentiation, BAP1/TP53 co-mutation, pN1 with extranodal extension, and prior progression on ipi/nivo at 9 months.

Plan: Continue cabozantinib 40 mg orally once daily. I see no clinical or radiographic indication to change therapy at this time. I will plan the next restaging CT in approximately 3 months. Labs (CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH) in 6 weeks, with clinical follow-up in 6-8 weeks.

2. HAND-FOOT SYNDROME (GRADE 1, STABLE)

Continued improvement from Grade 2 — now tolerating cabo 40 mg with only Grade 1 HFS. Bilateral plantar involvement, mild, no limitations. She is doing well with the urea-based cream and cushioned footwear. No dose modification needed. Reinforce skin care regimen and avoidance of heat/prolonged pressure. Will continue to monitor.

3. DIARRHEA (GRADE 1, MANAGED)

Grade 1, stable. 1-2 loose stools above baseline, managed PRN with loperamide. She reports no significant disruption to daily activities. Dietary counseling reinforced — low-residue diet, adequate hydration. No dose modification indicated.

4. HYPERTENSION (GRADE 1-2, CONTROLLED)

BP today 134/82 on amlodipine 10 mg. Well-controlled. No further medication adjustments needed at this time. She will continue to monitor BP at home. If SBP >150 on repeat, consider adding ACEI or ARB (also potentially reno-protective given single kidney and CKD stage 3b).

5. HYPOTHYROIDISM (GRADE 2 irAE — STABLE ON REPLACEMENT)

TSH 3.1 on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily. She is clinically euthyroid. Will recheck TSH at next labs in 6 weeks. No adjustments needed.

6. ANEMIA (MILD, STABLE)

Hgb 10.8, mildly below normal but stable from prior. Likely multifactorial: chronic disease, post-nephrectomy, CKD contribution. No transfusion indicated. Iron studies not repeated today — were unremarkable previously. Will recheck at next lab draw.

7. WEIGHT LOSS / NUTRITIONAL STATUS

Approximately 4 kg total weight loss since TKI initiation, but weight has been stable over the last month per patient report and today's weight. Appetite reduced but she is eating. I have referred her to registered dietitian today to optimize nutritional support and address appetite. Will monitor weight at each visit.

8. FATIGUE / QUALITY OF LIFE

Moderate fatigue persisting, ECOG PS 1. She is engaging with integrative oncology for an exercise program and finds it somewhat helpful. Continue support in this area. Sleep disruption noted — counseled on sleep hygiene; if persistent, may discuss short-term pharmacologic support.

9. PSYCHOSOCIAL / GOALS OF CARE

Ms. Okonkwo is having ongoing discussions with her family about her diagnosis and trajectory. Social work follow-up is in place. At this visit, I reiterated my appreciation of her situation — stable disease on second-line therapy is a good place to be, but her disease remains incurable with currently available treatments. We discussed the importance of ongoing quality of life optimization and that palliative care consultation remains available to her when she is ready. She acknowledged this and asked that we revisit it at the next visit. I will place a soft referral to supportive/palliative care for when she is open to it.

10. UPCOMING
- Labs (CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH) in 6 weeks
- Follow-up clinic visit in 6-8 weeks for toxicity check
- Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis in ~3 months
- Dietitian referral placed today
- Consider renal function monitoring given CKD 3b on single kidney and VEGFR TKI
- No new systemic therapy changes today

Time spent: ~45 minutes, more than half in counseling/coordination.
Electronically signed by: [Attending Oncologist]
